messagesPage.subtitle: A virtual corkboard of birthday wishes, mining puns, and heartfelt sticky notes from the crew.

quizPage.title: The Truth Drill
quizPage.subtitle: One face, one question, one honest answer.

galleryPage.title: Memory Scape
galleryPage.subtitle: Excavated stills from the field — rotate, hover, and remember.

messages.author: Sam P.
messages.body: Happy Birthday to our favorite rockstar! May your day be as precious as a 24-carat find.

messages.author: Elena M.
messages.body: Don't work too hard at the site today. Remember to actually celebrate the milestone!

messages.author: Dad & Mom
messages.body: "Life is like mining; you have to go through a lot of dirt to find the gold. You're definitely the gold!"

messages.author: Jordan K.
messages.body: Drinks on me tonight! We're celebrating the best future engineer in the industry.

messages.author: Crew
messages.body: Keep digging deep, the best is yet to come!

messages.author: Mark T.
messages.body: I found this old photo of our first site visit. We look so young! Happy Birthday, partner.

messages.author: Professor Vance
messages.body: Another year, another layer of brilliance! Shine on.

questions.prompt: What's your most precious memory from this semester?

questions.title: The Blueprint
questions.prompt: Where do you see your career shaft leading next?

letterParagraphs: Building this digital space for you felt like excavating a mountain of gold. Every photo, every memory we've shared over these years is a testament to the person you are—steadfast, brilliant, and absolutely unique.
This vault is yours. It's a reminder that even when the work is hard and the engineering problems feel impossible, you have a foundation of love that's deeper than any mine on earth.

letters.subtitle: Confidential / Heartfelt

timeline.title: 2nd Grade
timeline.body: The first playground encounter. A shared box of crayons and the mining of a lifelong bond.

timeline.title: Senior Year
timeline.body: Nights spent studying for finals, finding support in the quiet of the library's engineering wing.

timeline.title: 3rd Year (Now)
timeline.body: Professional breakthroughs and personal milestones. The most precious era yet.
